commands.review_summary:
Adds one CONTRIBUTING.md bullet telling contributors to update the PR description when proof or review-state follow-up is requested.

PR surface: Docs +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a documentation clarification, not a runtime bug. Source inspection shows current main and v2026.6.8 lack the exact follow-up-update reminder while adjacent template and CI docs already establish the PR body as durable review state.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer wording review and routine CI completion.

target.title: [codex] docs: clarify PR body proof updates
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Clarifies that proof and review-state follow-up requested by ClawSweeper, Codex, Barnacle, or maintainers should be folded into the PR description, not left only in a new comment.

Why does this matter now?

- The PR template already treats the description as the durable review record, but `CONTRIBUTING.md` did not spell out this common follow-up path in the same place contributors read before opening a PR.

What is the intended outcome?

- Contributors and AI-assisted PR authors keep the **Real behavior proof** and **Current review state** sections current after review feedback.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not change ClawSweeper behavior, proof gates, review automation, or the PR template.

What does success look like?

- A contributor who receives a proof or review-state request knows to update the PR body and may leave a short comment pointing reviewers to the update.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the new bullet is clear, accurate, and placed in the right contributor workflow section.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

- None.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- None.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No public issue; this is a small contributor-guidance clarification based on observed PR follow-up behavior.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Documentation-only guidance for where contributors should put proof and review-state updates after bot or maintainer feedback.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows; no OpenClaw runtime behavior changed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `Select-String -Path CONTRIBUTING.md -Pattern "When ClawSweeper"`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal capture from the local PR branch:

```text
PS C:\oc-work\openclaw-pr-body-proof-guidance> Select-String -Path CONTRIBUTING.md -Pattern "When ClawSweeper"

CONTRIBUTING.md:110:- When ClawSweeper, Codex, Barnacle, or a maintainer asks for more proof or a review-state update,
edit the PR description instead of only replying in a new comment. Keep the **Real behavior proof** and **Current
review state** sections current; a short comment can point reviewers to the update, but the PR body is the durable
source of truth for maintainers, bots, and automated proof gates.

PS C:\oc-work\openclaw-pr-body-proof-guidance> git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
PS C:\oc-work\openclaw-pr-body-proof-guidance> git rev-parse HEAD
46007f2044916893791312e1ed9c312fbe9c5cbf
```

- Observed result after fix: `CONTRIBUTING.md` contains the new guidance line, and `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` completed with no output, indicating no whitespace errors in the docs diff.
- What was not tested: No app runtime, UI, provider, channel, or CI behavior was tested because this is a documentation-only change.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Runtime proof is not applicable to this docs-only change; the relevant proof is the rendered contributor guidance text and clean Markdown diff.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): `CONTRIBUTING.md` required real behavior proof in the PR body but did not explicitly tell contributors what to do when proof or review-state updates are requested after review feedback.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `Select-String -Path CONTRIBUTING.md -Pattern "When ClawSweeper"`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- None. This is a documentation-only clarification.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The first Real behavior proof check failed because this PR body described local inspection but did not include copied terminal output or another accepted proof payload.

If no test was added, why not?

- The change only edits contributor documentation.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No

What is the highest-risk area?

- Contributor wording could be too prescriptive or could imply comments are useless.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The wording still allows a short comment to point reviewers to the update; it only makes the PR body the durable source of truth.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review after CI reruns.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on the Real behavior proof workflow to rerun against the updated PR body, then maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed the Real behavior proof check failure by folding copied terminal proof into this PR description instead of adding it only as a comment.
target.author: brokemac79
